summary: The strongest recent heavy-ion items concern finite-density QCD equation-of-state workflows, baryon-stopping observables, hot-QCD screening, and rotating-plasma energy-loss theory. The strongest physics AI/ML items are methodological rather than domain-specific: interpretability and explainability standards, residual-aware neural PDE solvers, latent diffusion posterior sampling for PDE inverse problems, and reinforcement-learning parameter scans. Two papers are flagged for caution because their claims appear stronger than the evidence supplied by abstract-level screening.
strongest_paper: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
top_takeaway: MUSES/Calliope appears to be the most practically important item because it targets finite-density equation-of-state consistency, transport inputs, thermodynamic derivatives, and hydrodynamic-ready workflows for Beam Energy Scan modeling.

papers.title: Studying the QCD Matter produced in Heavy-Ion Collisions using the MUSES Calculation Engine
papers.authors_short: Johannes Jahan, Kevin P. Pala, Yumu Yang, Isabella Danhoni, Prachi Garella, et al.
papers.abstract: The paper presents Calliope modules for hot and dense matter equations of state, thermodynamic quantities, pressure Hessians, transport coefficients, consistent equation-of-state merging, hydrodynamic inversion, and hydrodynamic simulations at Beam Energy Scan energies with movable critical-point and critical-scaling effects.

papers.title: Axial-Vector Lattice Benchmarks Reveal a Common Medium Response of Meson Screening in Hot QCD
papers.authors_short: Jie Ren, Chen Chen, Fei Gao, Si-xue Qin
papers.abstract: The paper combines lattice-QCD benchmarks with a symmetry-preserving Dyson-Schwinger baseline for hot-QCD meson screening masses, using axial-vector data and vector screening masses to validate a common medium response.

papers.title: Holographic light-quark energy loss in a spinning plasma
papers.abstract: The paper studies falling-string and shooting-string probes in a spinning black-brane background and finds enhanced, anisotropic light-quark energy loss with increasing temperature and rotation/boost parameter.

papers.title: Rapidity-even directed flow splitting of protons and antiprotons as a probe of baryon stopping in relativistic heavy-ion collisions
papers.abstract: The paper uses 3+1D viscous hydrodynamics plus hadronic transport to connect proton-antiproton rapidity-even directed-flow splitting and its midrapidity curvature to baryon rapidity deposition and baryon diffusion at Beam Energy Scan energies.

papers.title: Interpreting "Interpretability" and Explaining "Explainability" in Machine Learning in Physics
papers.authors_short: Rikab Gambhir, Luisa Lucie-Smith, Jesse Thaler
papers.abstract: The review distinguishes interpretability as structural transparency from explainability as mapping model behavior onto scientific domain knowledge, emphasizing task specification and intervention plans for ML in physics.

papers.title: Error-Conditioned Neural Solvers
papers.authors_short: Haina Jiang, Liam Wang, Peng-Chen Chen, Min Seop Kwak, Seungryong Kim, Brian Bell, Jeong Joon Park
papers.abstract: The method feeds PDE residual fields back into a neural solver as direct inputs, learning iterative correction policies and reporting strong accuracy across four PDE families, including turbulent Kolmogorov flow.

papers.title: Latent Diffusion Posterior Sampling with Surrogate Likelihood Guidance for PDE Inverse Problems
papers.abstract: L-DPS combines a VAE, latent diffusion prior, diffusion posterior sampling, and differentiable neural surrogate likelihood guidance for high-dimensional PDE inverse problems, demonstrated on inverse Darcy flow under sparse noisy observations.

papers.title: Supercool with PPO: Exploring Supercooled Phase Transitions via Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors_short: Wan-Zhe Feng, Zong-Huan Ye, Zi-Hui Zhang
papers.abstract: The paper uses PPO reinforcement learning to search dark U(1)x parameter space for supercooled phase-transition benchmarks with detectable gravitational-wave signatures, comparing reward-guided scans to Monte Carlo scans.

papers.title: Kinetic freeze-out and diffusion dynamics in small-system asymmetric collisions at sqrt(sNN)=200 GeV in light of a generalized Fokker-Planck distribution
papers.authors_short: M. Waqas, Wolfgang Bietenholz, Khusniddin K. Olimov, Muhammad Ajaz, Jihane Ben Slimane, Laila A. Al-Essa, A. Haj Ismail
papers.abstract: The paper fits neutral-pion pT spectra in p-Al, p-Au, d-Au, and He3-Au collisions with a generalized Fokker-Planck solution and interprets fitted parameters as freeze-out, diffusion, and QGP-medium information.

papers.title: \chisao{}: A GPU-Native Parallel Optimizer for Multimodal Black-Box Functions via Convergence-Anticonvergence Oscillation
papers.abstract: The optimizer targets multimodal black-box function optimization with GPU-native population dynamics and reports strong mode recovery and speedups on synthetic benchmark functions.
